safetyChecks.action: Check and document as baseline bedside data for this workup. Hypotension or severe hypertension changes acuity, medication safety, fluid strategy, and escalation.
safetyChecks.rationale: Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Cushing's Syndrome.

safetyChecks.management_change: Hypotension or severe hypertension changes acuity, medication safety, fluid strategy, and escalation.
safetyChecks.limitations: Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.

requiredExam.label: Inspect skin for bruising

source.source_section: Cushing's Syndrome curated endocrine workup: required bedside maneuver
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

requiredExam.action: Use this maneuver to evaluate hypercortisolism, Cushing phenotype, or glucocorticoid excess pattern. Easy bruising supports hypercortisolism phenotype and changes testing threshold.
requiredExam.rationale: Checks complications or precipitating illness that change treatment intensity, prevention, and disposition for Cushing's Syndrome.
requiredExam.technique: Inspect exposed skin and body habitus for easy bruising, wide purple striae, supraclavicular fullness, and related Cushingoid features.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: When Cushing syndrome or glucocorticoid excess is possible
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: hypercortisolism, Cushing phenotype, or glucocorticoid excess pattern
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
requiredExam.management_change: Easy bruising supports hypercortisolism phenotype and changes testing threshold.
requiredExam.limitations: Inspect skin for bruising is supportive for Cushing's Syndrome, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

requiredExam.technique: Ask the patient to rise from a chair without using arms if safe, observing proximal strength and need for assistance.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: When Cushing syndrome or glucocorticoid myopathy is possible
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: hypercortisolism-related proximal myopathy and functional severity

conditionalExam.limitations: Palpate abdomen for tenderness is supportive for Cushing's Syndrome, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

conditionalExam.label: Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration

source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

conditionalExam.action: Add when vomiting, dehydration, poor intake, polyuria, hyperglycemia is present; this evaluates volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion. Dehydration changes fluid strategy, lab urgency, and monitoring needs.
conditionalExam.rationale: Targets volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Cushing's Syndrome.
conditionalExam.technique: Inspect oral mucosa and tongue moisture with attention to dry mucosa, cracked lips, and overall hydration context.
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: When vomiting, poor intake, polyuria, hyperglycemia, hypercalcemia, or hypernatremia is present
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: volume status, dehydration severity, or peripheral perfusion

conditionalExam.limitations: Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration is supportive for Cushing's Syndrome, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

conditionalExam.label: Assess lower leg edema

source.source_section: Cushing's Syndrome curated endocrine workup: conditional bedside maneuver add-ons
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

conditionalExam.action: Add when edema, heart failure, kidney disease, vte, severe cushing is present; this evaluates volume overload, venous disease, or medication/renal-cardiac complication. Edema changes volume, VTE, renal-cardiac, and medication-safety interpretation.
conditionalExam.rationale: Targets volume overload, venous disease, or medication/renal-cardiac complication; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Cushing's Syndrome.
conditionalExam.technique: Inspect and press over both lower legs or dependent areas for pitting, symmetry, tenderness, and proximal extent.
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: When severe Cushing syndrome, renal/cardiac disease, or medication effect could alter volume status
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: volume overload, venous disease, or medication/renal-cardiac complication
conditionalExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
conditionalExam.management_change: Edema changes volume, VTE, renal-cardiac, and medication-safety interpretation.
conditionalExam.limitations: Assess lower leg edema is supportive for Cushing's Syndrome, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.
